llm_result.summary: The case centers on non-restorative sleep with daytime fog, episodic cognitive lapses, reactive self-criticism, and rumination. The best leverage points are: stabilize circadian timing and sleep restoration (bio), reduce cognitive load and support attentional control (bio/psycho), implement cognitive-emotional regulation to curb self-criticism and rumination (psycho), boost daytime activity to improve energy and interrupt rumination (psycho), and foster social supports for accountability (social). The proposed predictors are modifiable, feasible in a typical clinical or coaching context, and designed to produce measurable shifts in C01–C05 over a 4–6 week horizon, using simple self-report and behavioral metrics. No unsafe or illegal guidance is provided; safety monitoring remains in place for worsening symptoms.

solutions.solution_candidate: Regularize sleep-wake timing with a fixed wake time and consistent bedtimes, paired with 10–30 minutes of morning light exposure to strengthen circadian entrainment.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Stabilizes circadian rhythms to improve perceived sleep restoration and daytime alertness, reducing nightly variability that contributes to morning fatigue and cognitive fog.
solutions.measurement: Daily sleep diary (bedtime, wake time, perceived restfulness 0–10), minutes of morning light exposure, 0–10 morning alertness scale; optional actigraphy.
solutions.safety_notes: No safety concerns. Adjust wake time if persistent insomnia or excessive sleepiness develops.
solutions.mapping_query: MAP to C01: circadian entrainment and restoration via consistent schedule + morning light; track with sleep diary and morning alertness.

solutions.solution_candidate: Enhance sleep quality by optimizing the sleep environment (darkness, cool temperature, quiet) and implementing a brief pre-sleep wind-down with screen-time reduction.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Improved sleep efficiency and reduced nocturnal awakenings contribute to a more restorative subjective sleep experience and better daytime functioning.
solutions.measurement: Evening environment checklist (darkness, temperature, noise) + nightly sleep quality rating (0–10) + morning alertness; optional sleep diary entries.
solutions.safety_notes: Monitor for sleep initiation difficulties if environment changes are too restrictive; adjust as needed.
solutions.mapping_query: MAP to C01: sleep quality restoration via environmental optimization; monitor with environment checklist and sleep quality ratings.

solutions.solution_candidate: Teach and practice compassionate self-talk and CBT-based cognitive reframing to counter self-criticism after lapses and to reduce escalation into global worry.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Improves emotion regulation and reduces the likelihood of spiraling into rumination and overall functioning concerns after cognitive lapses.
solutions.measurement: Post-lapse thought records (1–3 items), self-rated self-criticism 0–10, mini-Ruminative Thoughts scale weekly.
solutions.safety_notes: Provide supportive coaching; monitor for excessive rumination during early practice.
solutions.mapping_query: MAP to C04/C05: reduce self-criticism and rumination via cognitive reframing; assess with thought records and rumination items.

solutions.predictor_variable: Cognitive Offloading Tools + Brief Attentional Training
solutions.solution_candidate: Use external memory aids (checklists, reminders, task organization) plus short daily attentional training to reduce lapses in attention/memory.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Reduces cognitive load and supports ongoing task completion, lowering frequency of attentional lapses and related frustration.
solutions.measurement: Frequency of lapses logged; weekly brief cognitive task performance (e.g., simple attention task scores); self-reported attention difficulty (0–10).
solutions.safety_notes: Ensure tools are user-friendly to avoid added frustration; monitor for overreliance on external aids.
solutions.mapping_query: MAP to C03: lessen lapses via cognitive offloading and attention training; track via lapse logs and cognitive task performance.

solutions.predictor_variable: Structured Daytime Activity Scheduling + Light Aerobic Movement
solutions.solution_candidate: Implement a daily schedule with planned activities and brief aerobic movement to boost daytime energy and disrupt rumination.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Increases physiological arousal and engagement during the day, improving alertness and reducing time for rumination.
solutions.measurement: Daily activity log (planned vs. actual), energy/motivation rating (0–10), number of rumination episodes (brief check-ins).
solutions.safety_notes: Adjust activity intensity to fitness level; ensure gradual buildup to prevent fatigue.
solutions.mapping_query: MAP to C02/C05: improve daytime energy and reduce rumination through activity scheduling and light exercise; measure with daily logs and energy ratings.

solutions.solution_candidate: Establish brief, regular social check-ins or an accountability partner to provide support and reduce rumination
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Social support and accountability provide distraction from rumination and improve mood regulation via interpersonal processes.
solutions.measurement: Weekly check-in occurrence count; perceived social support 0–10; rumination episodes per week (self-report).
solutions.safety_notes: Ensure boundaries and privacy; offer alternatives if social contact increases distress.
solutions.mapping_query: MAP to C05: reduce rumination via social support; track with weekly checks and rumination counts.
